title: PV Sindhu Reaches Japan Open Semifinals After Okuhara Walkover
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler PV Sindhu advanced to the semifinals of a prestigious international tournament.
summary: PV Sindhu progressed to the Japan Open 2026 semifinals after her opponent Nozomi Okuhara withdrew, setting up a clash with Chen Yufei.

title: Sindhu vs Chen Yufei Head-to-Head Ahead of Japan Open Semifinal
indian_angle: This article provides crucial context for Indian fans ahead of PV Sindhu's important semifinal match against Chen Yufei.
summary: A detailed look at the head-to-head record between PV Sindhu and Chen Yufei is provided, offering insights before their Japan Open 2026 semifinal encounter.

title: Player Eligibility for World Junior Championships 2026 Announced
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has released the eligibility criteria for Indian players aspiring to compete in the BWF World Junior Championships 2026.
summary: BAI has issued a circular detailing player eligibility requirements for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026 to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: Bhubaneswar U-19 Tournament Becomes World Junior Championships Selection Event
indian_angle: The Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament has been designated as the selection event for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, directly impacting Indian junior players.
summary: The Haryana U-19 Ranking Tournament has been postponed, with the Bhubaneswar U-19 Ranking Tournament now serving as the sole selection event for the BWF World Junior Championships 2026.

title: Delhi World Championships Needs Young Fans for Badminton's Future
indian_angle: The article discusses the importance of attracting younger generations to badminton, especially with the World Championships 2026 being hosted in Delhi, India.
summary: An opinion piece highlights the need for the upcoming BWF World Championships in Delhi to engage Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences to secure the sport's future popularity in India.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Implemented in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring system will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships for junior age groups in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of the 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships.
